opSettings.tagsIntro: Mark your dishes with tags like “House special” or “Spicy”. They show up as chips next to the name for both guests and the kitchen.
opSettings.tagsEmptyRows: No tags yet. Add the first one below.
opSettings.tagsInvalidSlug: "{slug}" is not a valid slug. Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
opSettings.tagsHelp: The display name is what the guest sees (“Spicy”). The slug is the internal identifier stored on each dish — it's auto-filled from the name but you can edit it. If you rename the slug of a tag that's already assigned, the dishes that had it stop showing it.
opSettings.meserosIntro: Assign each waiter the tables they serve. They'll only see the marked ones in Floor, Charges, and Tables. If none are marked, the waiter sees all (useful for shifts with no zone split).
opSettings.meserosEmptyNoMeseros: You don't have any waiters yet. Ask support to create them from the admin with the Waiter role.
opSettings.meserosNoTablesPrefix: First create the restaurant's tables in
opSettings.mesasIntroPart1: Capacity, minimum spend, and deposit for each table. Capacity is used to show the right tables when booking; the minimum spend is shown to the guest. The
opSettings.mesasIntroPart2: is charged online when booking and applied to the bill on arrival (non-refundable if they don't show up). Leave it blank for tables without a deposit.
opSettings.mesasIntroEnableSuffix: so these values are used in the booking flow.
opSettings.mesasEmptyPrefix: No tables yet. Create tables from
opSettings.mesasDeleteConfirm: Delete table {name}? This can't be undone.
opSettings.mesasDeleteOpenOrder: The table has an open order. Close it before deleting.
opSettings.mesasDeleteHasHistory: The table has order history, so it can't be deleted (reports would be lost).
opSettings.datafonosIntro: Assign each card reader to the waiter who carries it. When that waiter charges a bill from their session, the system sends the amount straight to THEIR card reader without going through Floor.
opSettings.datafonosEmpty: No card readers linked to the business yet. They'll appear here once paired with Kushki (or created in mock mode from the admin).
opSettings.datafonosAssignedHint: When {name} charges with a card reader, the charge goes straight to this device.
opSettings.datafonosUnassignedHint: Unassigned: any waiter has to open Floor and pick the device manually.
opSettings.policiesIntro: Define how tips are split, how shifts are counted, and when the system considers a table at risk of leaving without paying.
opSettings.policiesTipsSharedSubtitle: Tips belong to the restaurant. The operator decides how to distribute them at closing. The waiter doesn't see personal tips in their 'Me' view.
opSettings.policiesTipsByWaiterSubtitle: Each tip is tied to the waiter who charged the bill. Everyone sees their daily total in 'Me'.
opSettings.policiesShiftsQuestion: Who opens and closes the shift?
opSettings.policiesShiftsGlobalSubtitle: One shift per restaurant, opened and closed by the operator. Cash reconciliation at the end of the day.
opSettings.policiesShiftsByWaiterSubtitle: Each waiter opens and closes their own shift from the app. Useful when long / short shifts rotate. Coexists with the venue shift.
opSettings.policiesWalkoutQuestion: When is a table at risk of leaving without paying?
opSettings.policiesWalkoutIntro: The Tables view colors each table by the time unpaid since you delivered all dishes (or since they requested the bill and nobody attended). Raise the number in long-lingering restaurants; lower it for fast turnover.
opSettings.policiesWalkoutFootnote: For bill requests or unattended waiter calls, the thresholds are halved (more urgent). A table still cooking is never at risk.
opSettings.policiesCompQuestion: Allow closing bills as complimentary (on the house)?
opSettings.policiesCompIntro: The waiter can close a bill as a courtesy ($0), without charging it, noting who it was given to. The dishes consume inventory but don't count as sales.
opSettings.policiesWalkoutRangeError: Walkout threshold must be between 1 and 180 minutes.
opSettings.policiesBusinessDayQuestion: When does the business day start?
opSettings.policiesBusinessDayIntro: If your venue closes after midnight, set the cutoff hour to just after closing (e.g. 05:00). That way a payment at 2 a.m. counts toward the previous night's business day and your daily reports don't get split at midnight.
opSettings.policiesBusinessDayFootnote: The business day runs from {hour}:00 to {hour}:00 the next day. It only affects per-day reports (payments, end-of-day, summary); each shift's count uses its own open time.
opSettings.policiesNoLocalKicker: Waiter shift without the venue's shift
opSettings.policiesNoLocalQuestion: What happens if a waiter opens their shift and the venue hasn't opened its own?
opSettings.policiesNoLocalIntro: A waiter's shift lives inside the venue's general shift (their float comes from the general drawer). Choose what to do when a waiter arrives before the venue opens.
opSettings.policiesNoLocalBlockSubtitle: The waiter can't open their shift until the manager opens the venue's general shift. They get a message asking for it.
opSettings.policiesNoLocalAutoSubtitle: When the waiter opens their shift, the venue's shift opens too. Each waiter's float is added to the venue's float (which has none of its own). Floats are adjusted afterward from the cash view.
opSettings.policiesNoLocalFootnote: Always-on rule: a waiter's float can never exceed the venue's; if the venue is at $0, so are the waiters.

opReservasCfg.title: Reservations
opReservasCfg.intro: Let your guests reserve a table from a link. Set the shifts when you take reservations, how long each one lasts, and whether they confirm automatically or you approve them.

opReservasCfg.enableBody: Turn on so your guests can reserve a table from a public link.
opReservasCfg.shareHint: Share it on WhatsApp, social media or your website.
opReservasCfg.googleBody: Add your reservation link to your Google profile so people who find you on Maps can book directly. Use this special link — reservations that come in through it are tagged as “Google Maps” so you can measure how many it brings.
opReservasCfg.googleStep1Post: (the one that controls your Maps listing).
opReservasCfg.googleStep3Post: (or “Links”), paste the link above as your reservation system.
opReservasCfg.googleStep4Pre: If you don't see the Reservations option, paste it in the
opReservasCfg.googleStep4Post: field or as a featured link.

opErp.helpGuideCloseBody: In Close you reconcile the shift's cash drawer: sales by payment method, tips, and count differences.
opErp.helpGuidePurchasesBody: In Purchases you upload a photo or PDF and the AI extracts supplier, ingredients, quantities and taxes for you to review before saving.
opErp.helpGuideInventoryBody: Add ingredients, build recipes, and the system deducts inventory on each sale to compute real cost (COGS).
opErp.helpGuideAccountingBody: The monthly P&L shows sales, COGS, expenses, taxes and profit. You can download the Excel for your accountant.
opErp.bulkSkippedMeasure: {count, plural, one {# supply didn't change dimension because it has a linked supplier} other {# supplies didn't change dimension because they have a linked supplier}}
opErp.measureHint: Quantities are stored in the base unit: grams, milliliters or units.
opErp.measureLockedHint: Can't be changed: this ingredient already appears in supplier price lists.
opErp.errNameTaken: An ingredient with that name already exists.
opErp.errMeasureLocked: The dimension is locked: this ingredient already has references.
opErp.cardInsumosSubtitle: Raw materials: the base catalog for inventory, purchasing and recipes
opErp.badgeInsumos: {count, plural, =0 {No ingredients} one {# ingredient} other {# ingredients}}
opErp.proveedoresIntro: Who you buy from: contact details, payment terms and each supplier's price list.
opErp.emptySuppliersBody: Add the first one: a name and a phone number are enough to get started.
opErp.itemCount: {count, plural, =0 {no ingredients} one {# ingredient} other {# ingredients}}
opErp.paymentTermsHint: Days to pay invoices; empty = cash.
opErp.errSupplierNameTaken: A supplier with that name already exists.
opErp.priceListEmpty: This supplier has no ingredients on its price list yet.
opErp.contentHint: How much ONE presentation holds, in the unit you pick.
opErp.errAlreadyListed: That ingredient is already on this supplier's list.
opErp.confirmDeleteItem: Remove this ingredient from the price list?
opErp.cardProveedoresSubtitle: Contacts, payment terms and price lists for purchasing
opErp.badgeProveedores: {count, plural, =0 {No suppliers} one {# supplier} other {# suppliers}}
opErp.inventarioIntro: Stock on hand valued at average cost, plus the movement ledger of your ingredients.
opErp.emptyStockBody: Create your ingredients under Settings → Ingredients and record the first entry.
opErp.entryCostHint: Without a cost, the entry is valued at the current average cost.
opErp.adjustNoteHint: Recommended: leave a note explaining why you are adjusting.
opErp.errIngredientInactive: This ingredient is inactive; reactivate it to record movements.
opErp.newCountHint: The theoretical stock is frozen when the session is created: today's movements won't shift it while you count. You can save the draft and resume later.
opErp.closeCountConfirm: Closing will generate an inventory adjustment for every difference between theoretical and counted. Uncounted items are left untouched. Close the count?
opErp.closeCountResult: {count, plural, =0 {Count closed with no adjustments.} one {Count closed: # adjustment generated.} other {Count closed: # adjustments generated.}}
opErp.summaryCounted: {count, plural, =0 {No items counted} one {# item counted} other {# items counted}}
opErp.summaryWithDiff: {count, plural, =0 {no differences} one {# with a difference} other {# with differences}}
opErp.errCountOpenExists: There is already an open count: close it before creating another.
opErp.errNoIngredients: There are no active items to count with that filter.
opErp.errAlreadyClosed: This count has already been closed.
opErp.errQtyInvalid: Some counted quantities are invalid: check the flagged values.
opErp.reorderPointHint: When stock falls to this level or below, the ingredient is flagged "low stock" in Inventory. Empty = no alert.
opErp.reorderQtyHint: How much to order once the point is reached; empty = enough to cover it.
opErp.reorderErrInvalid: Invalid reorder point or quantity to order for that unit.
opErp.reorderBannerCount: {count, plural, one {# ingredient below its reorder point} other {# ingredients below their reorder point}}
opErp.comprasIntro: Purchase orders to your suppliers: create the order, send it and receive the goods.
opErp.emptyOrdersBody: Create your first order to request goods from a supplier.
opErp.noPriceListHint: This supplier has no price list: add lines without a presentation.
opErp.noPriceListMatches: No matches in the price list.
opErp.expectedCostHint: Prefilled with the last price; adjust it if it changed.
opErp.noLinesYet: Add at least one line to the order.
opErp.invTotalMismatch: The computed total ({computed}) does not match the invoice total ({printed}) — check the lines.
opErp.purchaseIvaDeductibleHint: When on, purchase VAT does not go into the inventory cost (it is recorded separately). When off, VAT is part of the cost (what you pay).
opErp.receptionsCount: {count, plural, one {# reception recorded} other {# receptions recorded}}
opErp.confirmCancelOrder: Cancel this purchase order? This action cannot be undone.
opErp.errWrongStatus: The order changed status: reload the page and try again.
opErp.errSupplierItemMismatch: One of the presentations does not belong to this supplier.
opErp.errNoLines: The order needs at least one line.
opErp.errLineInvalid: There is an invalid line: check quantities and costs.
opErp.receiveHint: Check what arrived and enter the quantity and the actual invoice cost for each line. You can receive in several deliveries.
opErp.errNothingToReceive: There is nothing pending to receive.
opErp.waNoPhoneHint: The supplier has no phone on file.
opErp.confirmDeletePayment: Delete this payment? The accounts-payable balance will be recalculated.
opErp.errExceedsBalance: The amount exceeds the outstanding balance.
opErp.suggestedHint: Ingredients below their reorder point, grouped by preferred supplier. Adjust the presentations and create one draft PO per supplier.
opErp.suggestedEmpty: No ingredients are below their reorder point.
opErp.suggestedUnassignedHint: These ingredients are low on stock but left out of the suggestion: mark a preferred supplier in the supplier’s price list to include them.
opErp.suggestedCreateFailed: Could not create the order for {name}.
opErp.suggestedNeedsInventory: Suggestions compare stock against the reorder point: enable the Inventory module to use them.
opErp.invUploadReadingHint: This may take up to a minute. Don't close this screen.
opErp.invUploadErrNoFile: No file received. Please try again.
opErp.invUploadErrBadFormat: Invalid format: upload a PDF or an image (JPG, PNG, WebP).
opErp.invUploadErrBadSize: The file is too large (max 15 MB) or empty.
opErp.invUploadErrModuleDisabled: The Purchasing module is turned off.
opErp.invUploadErrFailed: Couldn't read the invoice. Try another photo or upload a sharper one.
opErp.invReviewHint: Check what the AI read against the image and fix anything needed. Nothing is created until you confirm.
opErp.invReviewSummaryNew: {count, plural, one {# to create} other {# to create}}
opErp.invReviewDocCuentaCobroHint: The supplier is whoever gets paid (the "pay to" party).
opErp.invTaxRetentionNote: Withholdings are subtracted from the amount due.
opErp.invReviewConfirmDiscard: Discard this invoice? The reading will be lost.
opErp.invReviewErrNoLineIngredient: Every line needs an ingredient (existing or a new name).
opErp.invReviewErrLineQty: A line has an invalid quantity: check the amounts.
opErp.invReviewErrLineCost: A line has an invalid cost.
opErp.invReviewErrNoSupplier: Choose an existing supplier or enter a new supplier name.
opErp.invReviewErrNoLines: The invoice needs at least one line.
opErp.invReviewErrInvalid: Missing data or an invalid value: check the lines.
opErp.invReviewErrIngredientNotFound: One of the ingredients no longer exists.
opErp.invReviewErrNotPending: This invoice was already confirmed or discarded.
opErp.recetasIntro: Each dish's recipe with its real cost, food cost and margin against the menu price.
opErp.emptyDishesTitle: No dishes on the menu yet
opErp.emptyDishesBody: Create dishes in Menu and come back to build their recipes.
opErp.recipeLinesHint: Net quantities to the plate; waste adjusts the cost to gross.
opErp.modRecipeHint: Add or remove ingredients based on the option the diner picks (extra cheese, no onion…). Affects consumption and food cost.
opErp.noCostHint: This ingredient has no cost source: record inventory or a supplier price.
opErp.confirmDeleteRecipe: Delete this dish's recipe? It will be left without costing.
opErp.errWasteInvalid: Invalid waste: use a whole number between 0 and 90.
opErp.errDuplicateIngredient: There is a duplicate ingredient in the recipe.
opErp.subRecipesEmptyBody: Create the recipe of a prepared ingredient (sauces, doughs) to cost it per batch.
opErp.subRecipeOutputHint: The ingredient this preparation produces; create it in the catalog first.
opErp.subRecipeYieldHint: How much one full preparation produces (e.g. 2 L of sauce).
opErp.subRecipeLinesHint: Net quantities into the preparation; waste adjusts the cost to gross.
opErp.confirmDeleteSubRecipe: Delete this sub-recipe? The ingredient will lose its derived cost.
opErp.errSubRecipeCycle: Couldn't save: this sub-recipe would create a cycle between preparations (each using the other).
opErp.errIngredientInOwnRecipe: The prepared ingredient can't be in its own recipe.
opErp.engineeringActionPlowhorse: Popular but low-margin: raise the price or cut the cost.
opErp.engineeringActionPuzzle: Profitable but rarely sold: promote them.
opErp.engineeringActionDog: Neither popular nor profitable: rethink them or take them off the menu.
opErp.engineeringUnitsSold: {count, plural, one {# unit sold} other {# units sold}}
opErp.engineeringEmptyBody: The matrix needs dishes with a complete recipe and sales in the period.
opErp.fcAlertBannerCount: {count, plural, one {# dish with high food cost} other {# dishes with high food cost}}
opErp.priceAlertNoDishes: No dishes with a recipe use it.
opErp.productionIntro: Log the batches of your sub-recipes: ingredients leave inventory and the prepared item comes in at its real cost.
opErp.productionEmptyBody: Log the first one with the Produce button. Sub-recipes are created in Recipes.
opErp.productionSubRecipeHint: The prepared item that was produced — only sub-recipes with a yield.
opErp.productionNoSubRecipes: No sub-recipes to produce. Create them in Recipes, with their batch yield.
opErp.productionQtyHint: What the batch actually yielded — it doesn't have to match the exact yield.
opErp.productionLineNoCostHint: No cost in inventory: this output is valued at zero.
opErp.productionPartialHint: Some ingredients have no cost in inventory: the batch will come in with partial cost.
opErp.productionErrQtyInvalid: The quantity produced is not valid.
opErp.productionErrNoSubRecipe: That ingredient has no sub-recipe with a yield.
opErp.attReportHint: Pick a date range and download the CSV (opens in Excel).
opErp.accountingIntro: Business expenses, the monthly P&L and the books for your accountant.
opErp.expensesEmptyBody: Log rent, payroll, utilities… and the P&L deducts them for you.
opErp.recurringDayHint: It will be created automatically every month on that day.
opErp.confirmDeleteExpenseTemplate: Delete this recurring template? Expenses already created in past months are kept.
opErp.errExpenseInvalid: Check the fields: some values are invalid.
opErp.errRecurringDayRequired: Pick the day of the month (1-28) for the recurring expense.
opErp.errRecurringDayForbidden: The day of the month only applies to recurring expenses.
opErp.pnlCmvHint: No consumption recorded this month yet: enable inventory and recipes to compute real COGS. Purchases don't count toward the result.
opErp.taxConfigHint: The tax is embedded in the menu price (not added on top). It only feeds accounting and reports.
opErp.repDownloadHint: One file with Sales, Withholdings, Cost of sales and Inventory.
opErp.bookOrdersCount: {count, plural, =0 {No orders} one {# order} other {# orders}}
opErp.bookPurchasesCount: {count, plural, =0 {No orders received} one {# order received} other {# orders received}}
opErp.staffIntro: Plan your team's week, track attendance and keep labor cost in check.
opErp.staffWeekMissingSalary: {count, plural, one {# shift for an employee with no salary} other {# shifts for employees with no salary}}
opErp.staffConfirmCopyWeek: Copy last week's shifts into this week? Shifts that overlap existing ones are skipped.
opErp.staffCopyWeekResult: {copied, plural, one {# shift copied} other {# shifts copied}} · {skipped, plural, one {# skipped} other {# skipped}}
opErp.staffWeekEmptyBody: Create the first shift or copy the previous week.
opErp.shiftConfirmClearPunch: Clear this shift's actual clock-in and clock-out?
opErp.shiftTodayEmptyBody: Add an extra shift if someone came in to work.
opErp.staffTeamEmptyBody: Add your team members to plan their shifts.
opErp.staffConfirmDeactivate: Deactivate this employee? They no longer appear in the planner; their past shifts are kept.
opErp.staffConfirmDeleteEmployee: Delete this employee PERMANENTLY? They are removed along with all their shift and attendance history. If they just stopped working, deactivate them instead (history is kept).
opErp.errShiftRange: A shift must last between 15 minutes and 16 hours.
opErp.errShiftOverlap: It overlaps another shift for that employee on that day.
opErp.errPunchInvalid: The actual clock-out must be after the clock-in.
opErp.errEmployeeNameTaken: An employee with that name already exists.
opErp.errStaffUserNotFound: That user doesn't belong to your business.
opErp.laborMissingSalaryEmployees: {count, plural, one {# employee without a salary} other {# employees without a salary}}
opErp.kioskIdleHint: Tap a button to clock with your face.
opErp.kioskCameraHint: Center your face inside the circle.
opErp.kioskPhotoNotice: The photo is kept as evidence of the punch.
opErp.kioskCameraDenied: No camera access — clock by picking your name.
opErp.kioskNoMatch: We didn't recognize you — pick your name.
opErp.kioskNoFace: We couldn't see your face in the photo — pick your name.
opErp.kioskFaceUnavailable: Face recognition is unavailable — pick your name.
opErp.kioskNoEmployees: There are no active employees to clock.
opErp.kioskImplicitShift: We created your shift for today automatically.
opErp.kioskErrNoOpenShift: You don't have an open clock-in — clock in first.
opErp.templateSectionHint: The employee's default shifts — used by "Apply template" and for the kiosk's automatic shift.
opErp.templateApplyConfirm: Fill this week with the team's template schedules? Shifts that overlap with what's already planned are skipped.
opErp.templateApplyResult: {created, plural, one {# shift created} other {# shifts created}} · {skipped, plural, one {# skipped} other {# skipped}}
opErp.templateInvalid: Check the template ranges: each one must last between 15 minutes and 16 hours.
opErp.faceStatusNone: No face registered — this employee clocks in by picking their name on the kiosk.
opErp.faceEnrollHint: Take 1 to 3 photos of the face, front-facing and in good light.
opErp.faceNoFace: We didn't see a face in the photo — try again with better light.
opErp.faceCameraDenied: No camera access. Enable it in the browser to register the face.
opErp.faceUnavailable: Face recognition is not available on this device.
opErp.faceConsentLabel: I authorize the registration and use of my facial image solely for attendance control at this establishment. I can request its deletion at any time.
opErp.faceConsentRequired: You must accept the consent to save the face registration.
opErp.faceDeleteConfirm: Delete the face registration? The descriptors, reference photos and consent will be erased.
opErp.staffSettingsStrictHint: A past shift with no clock-in counts as an absence and costs $0.
opErp.staffSettingsPctHint: 75 = a 75% surcharge on the regular hourly rate.
opErp.staffSettingsPctInvalid: The surcharge must be a whole number between 0 and 200.
opErp.staffSettingsDivisorHint: The hourly value is salary / divisor. 240 = a traditional schedule (30 days × 8 h).
opErp.staffSettingsDivisorInvalid: The divisor must be a whole number between 1 and 1000.
opErp.reviewsHint: Punches made by picking the name by hand because facial recognition didn't identify the person. Check that the photo matches who they say they are.
opErp.reviewsFacialMismatch: Facial recognition didn't identify them — check that the photo is of this person.
opErp.reviewsMismatchNote: If the photo doesn't match, you can edit or delete the shift from the Week planner.
opErp.impInvIntro: Upload a photo or PDF of your catalog, or an Excel/CSV. The AI cleans up the names, infers the unit and category, and builds the starting stock so you just review it.
opErp.impInvFileHint: Photo or PDF of the list, or Excel/CSV. Max. 15 MB.
opErp.impInvInstructionsHint: E.g.: “the PRICE column is the cost without tax” or “the ones starting with LIQUOR are bottles”.
opErp.impInvInstructionsPlaceholder: Type any hint that helps read the catalog better here…
opErp.impInvReadingHint: It may take 30 to 60 seconds. Don't close this screen.
opErp.impInvReprocessWarn: Re-processing discards the edits you made in the table and haven't confirmed yet.
opErp.impInvErrNoFile: No file received. Please try again.
opErp.impInvErrBadFormat: Invalid format: upload a PDF, an image (JPG, PNG, WebP), or an Excel/CSV.
opErp.impInvErrBadSize: The file is too large (max. 15 MB) or empty.
opErp.impInvErrBadSheet: Couldn't read the Excel. Check that it has data in the first sheet.
opErp.impInvErrModuleDisabled: The Inventory module is turned off.
opErp.impInvErrFailed: Couldn't read the file. Try another photo or upload a sharper one.
opErp.impInvErrInvalid: Data is missing or a value is invalid: check the rows.
opErp.impInvErrIngredientNotFound: One of the supplies no longer exists. Process the file again.
opErp.impInvNoRows: The AI found no supplies in the file. Try another photo or add instructions.
opErp.impInvSummaryExisting: {count, plural, one {# already exists} other {# already exist}}
opErp.impInvUpdateExisting: Update stock for the ones that already exist
opErp.impInvUpdateExistingHint: When on, matched supplies also get the starting stock seeded from this import.
opErp.impInvDone: {created, plural, one {# created} other {# created}} · {seeded, plural, one {# with starting stock} other {# with starting stock}}
opErp.impInvErrNoneSelected: Select at least one supply to import.
opErp.impInvErrRowName: There's an included row with no name.
opErp.impInvErrRowQty: There's a row with an invalid quantity or unit.
opErp.impInvErrRowsInvalid: {count, plural, one {# row has an invalid quantity or unit — fix it or uncheck it.} other {# rows have an invalid quantity or unit — fix them or uncheck them.}}
opErp.impInvIssueQty: Invalid quantity. Check the number: it may be too small or have more than 3 decimals.
opErp.impInvIssuesChip: {count, plural, one {# with a problem} other {# with a problem}}
opErp.impInvExcludeIssues: Uncheck the ones with a problem
opErp.impInvRowsIncluded: {count, plural, one {# row included} other {# rows included}}

opDian.title: DIAN electronic invoicing
opDian.intro: Set up electronic invoicing directly with DIAN: upload your digital certificate, load your portal credentials, and run the enablement test set.
opDian.masterKeyNotReady: The server is not ready to store credentials securely yet. Please contact the administrator.
opDian.emisorMissing: Issuer data is missing before you can invoice: {fields}.
opDian.emisorMissingHint: These fields are set under the business Identity settings.
opDian.certHelp: .p12 file issued by an authorized certification authority (Certicámara, GSE, Andes SCD). It signs your invoices and authenticates the channel with DIAN.
opDian.credsHelp: Software ID, PIN, and technical key from the DIAN portal, plus the test set identifier. Stored secrets are never shown; leave a field empty to keep it unchanged.
opDian.credsSoftwareIdHint: Identifier of the own software registered in the DIAN portal.
opDian.credsTechnicalKeyHint: Technical key of the numbering resolution; it is part of the CUFE calculation.
opDian.credsUnchangedHint: Configured. Leave it empty to keep the current value.
opDian.credsTestSetHint: Identifier of the DIAN portal test set (required for enablement).
opDian.credsEnvHint: Use Testing while you complete the tests; switch to Production once DIAN enables you.
opDian.habHelp: Run the test set against the testing environment. DIAN validates the documents asynchronously; it may take a few seconds.
opDian.habNotReady: Complete the certificate, credentials, and Test Set ID in the Testing environment to run the test set.
opDian.errModuleDisabled: The electronic invoicing module is not active for this business.
opDian.errNoEmisor: The business issuer could not be resolved.
opDian.errInvalid: Invalid data. Check the fields and try again.
opDian.errBadSize: The file exceeds the allowed size (512 KB) or is empty.
opDian.errNoKey: The certificate does not contain a valid private key.
opDian.errNoCert: The file does not contain a valid certificate.
opDian.errNoTestSet: The Test Set ID is missing to run the enablement.
opDian.errNotHabilitacion: The test set only runs in the Testing environment.
opDian.errEmisorIncomplete: Issuer data is missing (resolution or prefix).
opDian.errNoCertificate: No digital certificate has been uploaded.
opDian.errDecryptFailed: The stored credentials could not be decrypted.
opDian.errGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
